name: google-earth
description: Google Earth restoration — probe diagnostics and experimental auth bypass. Based on reverse engineering of gemmi_final, libembeddedearth.so, and live server testing showing kh.google.com still serves tile data and /geauth has been removed.
author: dspl1236
scripts: ge_probe.sh - Probe GEMMI state, check binaries, network, EOL flags (safe, read-only)
ge_deploy.sh - Deploy GEMMI binaries from SD card to EFS (for cars without GE)
ge_enable.sh - EXPERIMENTAL: Add disableAuthKey to drivers.ini (requires reboot)
ge_restore.sh - Revert all changes made by ge_enable.sh
risks: EXPERIMENTAL: Based on binary analysis, NOT car-tested.
ge_enable.sh modifies drivers.ini on writable EFS (backed up before change).
ge_enable.sh does NOT modify IFS, lsd.jxe, or any firmware files.
The disableAuthKey parameter was found in libembeddedearth.so strings — its effect is theoretical.
Google may change their server behavior at any time.
research: kh.google.com/dbRoot.v5 returns HTTP 200 (16KB config) — server is alive
kh.google.com/geauth returns HTTP 404 — auth endpoint REMOVED
geFreeLoginServer found next to kh.google.com in binary
Audi NAR (HU_VARIANT=6) has NO RANGE_ block on Google Earth
gemmi_final runs as standalone QNX process, independent of EOL flags
